compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can assess ticket dispatch readiness via ticket quality-check <id>
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `quality-check` subcommand to the ticket CLI dispatcher that wraps issue-quality-check.sh's dispatch-readiness validation.

**Why**: The ticket system should answer "is this ticket ready for agent dispatch?" without callers invoking a separate script.

**Scope**:
- IN: New `quality-check` case in `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` dispatcher routing to issue-quality-check.sh logic; integration test using a synthetic fixture exercising the subcommand through the dispatcher asserting exit codes and output match issue-quality-check.sh
- OUT: Migrating callers (Epic e1b2-2653); removing issue-quality-check.sh; changing quality scoring logic

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket quality-check <id>` is callable via the dispatcher and produces the same exit codes and output as issue-quality-check.sh
  <- Satisfies: "quality-check (readiness validation for issue-as-prompt dispatch -- replaces issue-quality-check.sh)"
- When this story is complete, the integration test exercises quality-check through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture and asserts output equivalence with issue-quality-check.sh
  <- Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test asserting output equivalence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] This story depends on S4 (8e33-a212) landing first to avoid parallel dispatcher edits

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
